preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, with South Africa given a 13% chance of victory. That 49-percentage-point gap reflects a clear asymmetry in expected strength on a neutral pitch.

The draw carries modest weight at 26%, suggesting the match has scope for a competitive, goalless outcome or a low-scoring stalemate. South Korea's win probability substantially exceeds the combined underdog chances, but the draw probability—roughly one-quarter—indicates the result remains genuinely uncertain rather than predetermined.

Model and sportsbook consensus align closely across all three outcomes. South Korea sits at 62% versus 59% in the market, South Africa at 13% versus 17%, and the draw at 26% versus 24%. Gaps are under 4 percentage points throughout, signaling broad agreement on the match's likely character.
